examples: When tucking into a meal in Egypt, by-pass the saltshaker.
Three-quarters of the sodium in our diets isn't from the saltshaker.
Night had fallen, and I went out under this great overturned saltshaker of stars, and I could see the tail lights of cars disappearing around the headlands 12 miles to the south.
On Star Trek, Dr. "Bones" McCoy could diagnose cancer just by waving a saltshaker--sorry, a "medical tricorder"--over a patient's body.
And American chef Dan Perlman crafts his thematic menus for Casa Saltshaker with a Mediterranean base and tailors them to coincide with upcoming holidays.
